title: Spicy Crumbly Dutch Apricot Crumble
description: A romantic Dutch dessert featuring apricots, a hint of spice, and a crumbly texture, perfect for a special occasion. This low-carb-friendly recipe incorporates fermented ingredients for added depth of flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the sliced apricots, granulated sweetener, and fermented ginger.
In a separate bowl, combine the almond flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cayenne pepper.
Add the melted butter, beaten egg, and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients and mix until crumbly.
Transfer the apricot mixture to a 9x9-inch baking dish.
Top the apricots with the crumbly mixture, spreading it evenly.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the fruit is tender.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh apricot slices if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
